                                        120.Origin_Intro_Laezel: One question of many, I promise you.

                                        Metadata

                                        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 30d6076a-ded8-df75-8491-b092b26f35cdSpeaker: 2 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h6235ac6cg8ef3g4ee2g875agcb004c566f67Text UUID: 7ac20960-3e9b-4167-bafc-3a3d78ac0c7dTimeline Phase Index: 126Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 30d6076a-ded8-df75-8491-b092b26f35cdTimeline Phase Duration: 3.59sTimeline Phase Shots: 1
                                        • +
                                          399.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Do it. Take his mind, and be quick.

                                          Metadata

                                          • +
                                            555.Origin_Intro_Laezel: When the brain is dead, I go my way - you go yours.

                                            Metadata

                                            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: fe38e0e5-7148-c7c9-caf9-7c18de62b0b9Speaker: 2 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h47be872agcde5g4d76g8a70g3242c6ab66c6Text UUID: 33b9746a-7a1e-484f-b6b2-c65287b8aa4eGroup ID: af214585-0245-6aba-31bb-3879065fbb7aGroup Index: 1
                                            • +
                                              446.No Speaker: (no line text)note: Player mind flayer does a hand wave gesture and removes the globe of domination around Orpheus. The Player (as a mind flayer) wraps their tentacles around Orpheus' face and brings their mouth over Orpheus' head. Player feeds CLUMSILY. Suckling noises. Orpheus' eyes dart around in pain as his brain is consumed. Once it's done, Orpheus' head flops to the side, his head empty. END_IllithidOptions_State_ExitPortalAvailableEND_IllithidOptions_Event_PlayerOpenedExitPortalEND_IllithidOptions_State_MiniDominationOrbRemovedEND_IllithidOptions_State_AssimilatedOrpheus

                        43.Player: You are a fool. Free him, and he's bound to kill us.

                        Metadata

                        Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 1377a6dd-b5bb-453b-8d01-274f1413faf9Speaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: hb6f57e21gfa75g4887g8924gbd7169a240abText UUID: 78223b51-167d-468c-902f-cf2ec4717387Show Once: True
                        • +
                          401.Origin_Intro_Laezel: A true coward's words. Do you trust me so little to think I'd lead you to death's maw?

                          Metadata

                          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: b08cda72-04a2-b651-02ab-727e13383c86Speaker: 2 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h161aa68fgc716g48e0ga1d9g1d1d2d5fc4adText UUID: dd2c983b-5848-4c98-b0c2-d8fb51015be4Timeline Phase Index: 36Timeline Dialog Node UUID: b08cda72-04a2-b651-02ab-727e13383c86Timeline Phase Duration: 7.22sTimeline Phase Shots: 2
                          • See Node 344 (Origin_Intro_Laezel) And to think - you were the source of my joy. I trusted you.

                        233.Player: I will not betray Vlaakith. The traitor prince dies by my hand!Githyanki

                        Flags and Tags

                        ruletagtype rule
                        • Githyanki {e49c027c-6ec6-4158-9afb-8b59236d10fd}

                        Text

                        Variant 1

                        combine=0
                        • Githyanki
                        I will not betray Vlaakith. The traitor prince dies by my hand!

                        Variant 2

                        Join me or get out of my way, Lae'zel.
                        h5757b8eegf947g4d03g9ccdg4c64e38b1e7e | 0afd554f-9d7b-46de-98c0-200a2165fd56 | stub

        92.Narrator: *As the githyanki prince takes his blade, a silent cry pierces your head - a psionic scream to the Astral Plane.*note: Orpheus, freed, takes his shortsword out of the rock nearby and wields it, threateningly, ready to strike.Githyanki

                      Variant 2

                      It's Lae'zel's word that's kept you from losing your mind, so show some gratitude.
                      h5f3c1df2gccb5g4e74gadaeg2cbf339478ad | f9b2ecb7-9e1e-4ccd-b26d-4651f8c63d21 | stub

                                  45.Speaker 0: This is the stuff of fables.

                                  Metadata

                                  Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 13baa62e-34c7-42b4-877a-a0bca78cc2e7Speaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: h49c8d996ge29eg4f1cga595ge9beab490504Text UUID: 89d6c7de-b69b-42d3-ac7f-1ff14f0d902dTimeline Phase Index: 150Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 13baa62e-34c7-42b4-877a-a0bca78cc2e7Timeline Phase Duration: 40sTimeline Phase Shots: 10Group ID: 13baa62e-34c7-42b4-877a-a0bca78cc2e7Group Index: 0
                                  • +
                                    54.Speaker 0: It is as if I am reliving the legend of Oryndoll.

                                    Metadata

                                    Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 19a9cfe2-8fec-4488-3c16-5e098ad5d6d4Speaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: h586617beg80f8g4609ga867g6d6469a8a70eText UUID: 59ca0fc9-515d-4a0e-8d60-c35c3f06217bGroup ID: 13baa62e-34c7-42b4-877a-a0bca78cc2e7Group Index: 1
                                    • +
                                      353.Speaker 0: You are the illithid Urengol, rebelling against your own hivemind. And I am the noble githyanki Valraag who must now reconsider his position.

                                      Metadata

                                      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 9d377aab-3a76-12ba-4619-1e23633fbf12Speaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: h9d097169g7610g459ag80c7ge24495737f45Text UUID: 5dfe1b40-1d2d-45ea-ba99-94c41abd248eGroup ID: 13baa62e-34c7-42b4-877a-a0bca78cc2e7Group Index: 2
                                      • +
                                        178.Speaker 0: An illithid capable of rebelling against the Netherbrain's instruction... Not only capable, but willing...

                                        Metadata

                                        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 47f27d29-4e12-408f-0703-f175feb502c9Speaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: hfae6d9ecg8b47g4223g9b16g6fe25d901f04Text UUID: b74aaebc-843a-44ce-85b1-52036c76371dGroup ID: 13baa62e-34c7-42b4-877a-a0bca78cc2e7Group Index: 3
                                        • +
                                          310.Speaker 0: If your intentions are as righteous as they seem, then this is an advantage I cannot overlook. An advantage that must be grasped, for our enemy is formidable indeed.

                                          Metadata

                                          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 8738e0d4-d699-3efe-ed26-47b1517c99afSpeaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: hf570ff93gc9b1g4cc2g8794g8a54184a0c11Text UUID: 534e308b-5e54-4804-8fc3-9e12f8c1e5e1Group ID: 13baa62e-34c7-42b4-877a-a0bca78cc2e7Group Index: 4
                                          • +

                                                          • +
                                                            520.No Speaker: (no line text)note: Same as in END_IllithidOptions_Assimilation The Player (as a mind flayer) wraps their tentacles around Orpheus' face and brings their mouth over Orpheus' head. Player feeds CLUMSILY. Suckling noises. Orpheus' eyes dart around in pain as his brain is consumed. Once it's done, Orpheus' head flops to the side, his head empty. END_IllithidOptions_State_ExitPortalAvailableEND_IllithidOptions_Event_PlayerOpenedExitPortalEND_IllithidOptions_State_AssimilatedOrpheus

                                                                  36.Narrator: *Even at a distance you feel it - satisfaction. The feeding has nourished you - you are ready to face the Netherbrain again.*

                                                                  Metadata

                                                                  Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 11bc5b03-dc6a-b204-7348-f4abfb695623Speaker: -666 (Narrator)Text Handle: h58ea46acg0758g4e6ag8239g37ccbfc49af0Text UUID: b0814666-d15b-4490-8dd2-a66e41fbf747Group ID: b4d33c78-9faf-a2a7-3050-a05e6c4fbefeGroup Index: 2
                                                                  • •

      Metadata

    460.Narrator: *As the githyanki prince takes his blade, a silent cry pierces your head - a psionic scream to the Astral Plane.*note: Orpheus calmly pulls a shortsword out of the rock nearby - his sword that was placed just out of reach that he's been staring at the whole time he's been imprisoned. He looks threatening. Githyanki

    Flags and Tags

    ruletagtype rule
    • Githyanki {e49c027c-6ec6-4158-9afb-8b59236d10fd}

    Text

    Variant 1

    combine=0
    • Githyanki
    *As the githyanki prince takes his blade, a silent cry pierces your head - a psionic scream to the Astral Plane.*
    hd632ff79g1f80g4288g9f61gf565741ad889 | 7b995333-9a65-4474-9833-4ebcbe360c75 | stub

                            494.Speaker 0: Make no mistake - were it not for our common goal, I would strangle you where you stand. note: Orpheus looks at you with distaste before speaking

                            Metadata

                            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: de98e4fd-c7e2-80ed-5b61-cb137414ae58Speaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: hd8a45767g784eg4f23gb2feg4ae1535cbe3fText UUID: 95f9f0db-b057-4b85-abf5-d1f1bac69164Timeline Phase Index: 73Timeline Dialog Node UUID: de98e4fd-c7e2-80ed-5b61-cb137414ae58Timeline Phase Duration: 8.59sTimeline Phase Shots: 3
                            • +
                              458.Speaker 0: Your very existence tests my self-restraint.

                              Metadata

                              Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: cb7a728f-2d55-8562-5813-c13a5da21a06Speaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: hd1adaedagb3d8g4473gad08g1eaf2b857831Text UUID: a4e5b7ab-0d55-402a-96cb-8613449e42eeTimeline Phase Index: 100Timeline Dialog Node UUID: cb7a728f-2d55-8562-5813-c13a5da21a06Timeline Phase Duration: 4.61sTimeline Phase Shots: 1
                              • +
                                381.Speaker 0: I will neither forgive nor forget your abuse of my powers.

                                Metadata

                                Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: a90f9e6b-4249-d5ef-4229-c41ac8fc969fSpeaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: h49285666gc5c1g4403gaf58gf12a1153e221Text UUID: 8f87b855-413e-4b45-8e12-4d3b7d4c32d6Timeline Phase Index: 25Timeline Dialog Node UUID: a90f9e6b-4249-d5ef-4229-c41ac8fc969fTimeline Phase Duration: 6.99sTimeline Phase Shots: 2
                                • +
                                  237.Player: I had no choice. Without your powers, I would have died.

                                  Metadata

                                  Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 6408da73-29ed-b8c3-dbff-ff1426e10337Speaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: hf670c6d0g4c8ag448eg97eag328565be195dText UUID: 81a24bf7-74cb-4f67-bec9-5f73fee92058Show Once: True
                                  • +
                                    304.Speaker 0: That is true. And it would have been the honourable outcome for one destined to become ghaik.

                                    Metadata

                                    Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 84232789-5702-9a7d-7aa3-55fea5ea256dSpeaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: hb1174844g54e8g4358g80d1gb49757faaa7eText UUID: 3e3af2d8-bbf4-48f6-bc3d-621a4149bcefTimeline Phase Index: 42Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 84232789-5702-9a7d-7aa3-55fea5ea256dTimeline Phase Duration: 8.02sTimeline Phase Shots: 1
                                    • +

                                      • +
                                        370.Speaker 0: They would have freed me, and I would have stopped the elder brain before it evolved into a Netherbrain.

                                        Metadata

                                        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: a54a7a9f-4df2-dd39-7ccb-f4a95858e372Speaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: hd666a78ag00c3g4762gb564g432b2c51debeText UUID: 8465a17d-f5a5-49d1-b02c-268ad2cf5c3fTimeline Phase Index: 21Timeline Dialog Node UUID: a54a7a9f-4df2-dd39-7ccb-f4a95858e372Timeline Phase Duration: 6.4sTimeline Phase Shots: 1
                                        • +
                                          12.Speaker 0: All that suffering - avoidable. Were it not for the choices you made.

                                              252.Speaker 0: The ghaik was correct about one thing - the Netherbrain's power is beyond us. END_IllithidOptions_Knows_NeedMindFlayerOrpheus

                                              • +
                                                214.Speaker 0: The hardest metal in the world would not cut through its mind, for it is made of thought itself.

                                                Metadata

                                                Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 59aa93dc-243c-8ccf-8241-9b9175566addSpeaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: hbaee16d7gb1afg4245g9139g611453391af1Text UUID: 7a2e0185-66e5-4fd4-91ca-143acd9c09c5Group ID: 6d51e682-c865-f649-e23a-bec50280331cGroup Index: 1
                                                • +
                                                  308.Speaker 0: At this point, it will take an illithid to unleash the full potential of the Netherstones.

                                                  Metadata

                                                  Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 85027e28-6a4a-3185-f12e-707de23f9505Speaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: hb6837935gaa55g4a65gb019g7da931e46bdaText UUID: b93db984-29d5-49d4-b850-718d6b03832cGroup ID: 6d51e682-c865-f649-e23a-bec50280331cGroup Index: 2
                                                  • +
                                                    563.Gale: And it would take one wizard to unleash a cataclysm powerful enough to tear apart the brain's very being. We have no choice but to consider using the orb.ORI_Gale_State_WillingToDieGaleEND_IllithidOptions_State_GaleMentionedBomb

                                                    Flags and Tags

                                                    checktype Global
                                                    • ORI_Gale_State_WillingToDie {3f22f471-60e6-11df-1c95-19f756f81994} = True
                                                    checktype Tag | speaker 5 Gale
                                                    • Gale {9b0354c0-56d9-4723-8034-918ac9abab19} = True
                                                    settype Global
                                                    • END_IllithidOptions_State_GaleMentionedBomb {fe699768-f358-442f-8b53-2a0f163d3c73} = True

                                                    Metadata

                                                    Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: ffd106f5-ec64-ab33-5e51-b82740c7c24aSpeaker: 5 (Gale)Text Handle: h91ad9e34g7081g48e9gb0f2g52e0f477744fText UUID: bfaaa1c7-57ac-4cff-9f10-51e9aa97bc76Timeline Phase Index: 117Timeline Dialog Node UUID: ffd106f5-ec64-ab33-5e51-b82740c7c24aTimeline Phase Duration: 22.34sTimeline Phase Shots: 2Group ID: ffd106f5-ec64-ab33-5e51-b82740c7c24aGroup Index: 0
                                                    • +
                                                      89.Speaker 0: I do not know you, wizard, and I do not know this 'orb'.

                                                      Metadata

                                                      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 24312268-3c78-eea0-37f0-c4061f335694Speaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: ha2023301g0fa7g4b31g81e2g821b37f2f840Text UUID: f3f18789-94eb-48e7-8df3-3865797bb1d8Group ID: ffd106f5-ec64-ab33-5e51-b82740c7c24aGroup Index: 1
                                                      • +
                                                        364.Speaker 0: But what I do know is that we must not gamble with the fate of the world. I cannot simply depend upon your word.

                                                        Metadata

                                                        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: a24f207b-0561-757a-5216-51d395e501aeSpeaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: hbf9a8c84gccc5g45a3ga656g6cb4053c4d51Text UUID: a4e1bb66-d07e-48de-af46-878d023a8cbdGroup ID: ffd106f5-ec64-ab33-5e51-b82740c7c24aGroup Index: 2
                                                        • •

                                                  • +
                                                    391.Origin_Intro_Laezel: I've heard enough. My Queen demands your head. It is time I take it.!ORI_Laezel_State_IsOnOrpheusPath!AvatarLae'zel7ba0a452-73f7-68dc-cf1e-78c6c117cca5_LaezelVlaakith3

                                                    • +
                                                      290.Narrator: *Vlaakith's greatest threat stands before you. But to end him would condemn you and your allies to be the Netherbrain's eternal thralls.*!ORI_Laezel_State_IsOnOrpheusPathAvatarLae'zel

                                                      • +

                                                        • +
                                                          23.Speaker 0: Just as I was free...note: Orpheus looks down, pensive, sad - a long pause as he thinks about everything he wanted to do, everything he will be leaving behind, just as freedom was beckoning.

                                                          Metadata

                                                          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 0e0a45c7-219b-2726-a6d6-d588d4573b26Speaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: h42eda05dg3521g4ab4g9894g154d0545e999Text UUID: 116bf486-9be2-419c-8713-cd9c9e1851cdTimeline Phase Index: 142Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 0e0a45c7-219b-2726-a6d6-d588d4573b26Timeline Phase Duration: 3.49sTimeline Phase Shots: 2
                                                          • +
                                                            342.Speaker 0: I will do it.

                                                            Metadata

                                                            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 98214174-be9f-4cb0-6d7b-94e6fe9894feSpeaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: he36005e8g0afdg447ag9af1g122c15df22f8Text UUID: bd5805c8-9f77-46ff-b605-4cd376d02437Timeline Phase Index: 133Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 98214174-be9f-4cb0-6d7b-94e6fe9894feTimeline Phase Duration: 13.8sTimeline Phase Shots: 4Group ID: 98214174-be9f-4cb0-6d7b-94e6fe9894feGroup Index: 0
                                                            • +
                                                              112.Speaker 0: I will become - illithid. I will sacrifice my soul for my people. I will end the Grand Design.

                                                              Metadata

                                                              Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 2e633910-05f8-fafc-7464-d66f19489e31Speaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: ha66c747cg28c7g4600gb054gedfb62f34d69Text UUID: 2aea1331-b1d3-4a8d-97fc-b607ff5da981Group ID: 98214174-be9f-4cb0-6d7b-94e6fe9894feGroup Index: 1
                                                              • +
                                                                493.Origin_Intro_Laezel: And yet you will live. A ghaik thrall wearing the skin of his masters at long last.!ORI_Laezel_State_VlaakithPathOrpheusAlly!Lae'zel is on the Orpheus path.

                                                                • +
                                                                  560.Speaker 0: Is this what you think? That I am an agent of the Illithid Empire?

                                                                  Metadata

                                                                  Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: ff8eb585-49b7-bbd6-174c-2fd364dca5c5Speaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: hd3bf96aeg3d56g48a2gbd35g0ab2f7959ff0Text UUID: aa4e9d80-85d5-46b9-aa1e-087eed0d546eGroup ID: de5790ff-718f-3f21-ec40-61697ee61accGroup Index: 1Addressed Speaker: 2
                                                                  • +
                                                                    511.Origin_Intro_Laezel: It is etched in slate. So says Vlaakith, and so it must be.

                                                                    Metadata

                                                                    Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: e476cc0e-89eb-ee5d-8899-bb9f56c9ee7bSpeaker: 2 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h1c7b8f06g7fb9g407fg9e1bgf3a8af1ba419Text UUID: fb6a8aaa-42c6-4db0-a7af-75fe483717afGroup ID: de5790ff-718f-3f21-ec40-61697ee61accGroup Index: 2Addressed Speaker: 0
                                                                    • +
                                                                      232.Origin_Intro_Laezel: My mission is eternal, immutable. Our path ends here, k'chakhi. On this day, Gith's son shall perish by my hands.

                                                                      Metadata

                                                                                  423.Speaker 0: Even in my darkest hours, I knew it was my destiny to save my people - I could never have imagined this would be the way.note: Orpheus speaks while transforming into an Illithid.

                                                                                  Metadata

                                                                                  • +
                                                                                    484.Speaker 0: Gith'ka tavkim krash'ht.note: Orpheus lifts his head to the stars, utters a prayer, and becomes full Illithid.

                                                                                    Metadata

                                                                                      • +
                                                                                        457.Speaker 0: All to wield these... note: Transformation over. Orpheus is Illithid and speaks telepathically now (like the Emperor.) The stones float over into his grasp. He looks at them with dismay for a beat before the next line.                                                                         4.Speaker 0: G'lyck. Cursed to be in the presence of k'chakhi.

                                                                        Metadata

                                                                        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 02cb30a3-a284-14f7-7441-6142a533822bSpeaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: h84bf7a49gbbadg4d18g9f57g9defa6003000Text UUID: 61f25f05-5968-473f-8260-b73621ffb144Group ID: df6255c9-793c-ca37-d5d5-b54514b93c76Group Index: 1

                                                                      Metadata

                                                                      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 46c6d224-8f6e-e448-69e9-e61c4de45e63Speaker: 2 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h06d98f7ag3d39g4efeg81a2g7a0e714b3a5aText UUID: bf7e2295-7364-4f1d-8d95-5b199d6cf3ebTimeline Phase Index: 185Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 46c6d224-8f6e-e448-69e9-e61c4de45e63Timeline Phase Duration: 3.25sTimeline Phase Shots: 1Addressed Speaker: 1
                                                                      • +
                                                                        68.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Vlaakith's mission is eternal, immutable. Our path ends here. On this day, Gith's son shall perish by my hands.

                                                                        Metadata

                                                                        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 1de97960-9052-5d78-33d6-697d9882a67bSpeaker: 2 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h7efed2d7gdd3dg4e23gb82ag8a9bf658636fText UUID: b2442dd8-04f4-4154-b99f-57911859c382Timeline Phase Index: 146Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 1de97960-9052-5d78-33d6-697d9882a67bTimeline Phase Duration: 10.12sTimeline Phase Shots: 2
                                                                        • +
                                                                          398.Player: Respond with the tadpole. 'We need Orpheus' protection. You can kill him when the Netherbrain's dead. I promise.'ORI_Laezel_State_VlaakithPathOrpheusAlly

                                                                          settype Global

                                                                          • +
                                                                            94.Narrator: *A moment passes - and you feel Lae'zel relent.*

                                                                            Metadata

                                                                            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 250031e3-649a-f505-60e8-0529859f0210Speaker: -666 (Narrator)Text Handle: h96a3a603gf3e2g493cg9afeg68b572495e2bText UUID: 414636fd-df86-49db-91f9-617ff880dabdTimeline Phase Index: 113Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 250031e3-649a-f505-60e8-0529859f0210Timeline Phase Duration: 33.8sTimeline Phase Shots: 6Timeline Location Changes: Q Companion position (ff98d926-7773-4e8b-839a-2bc7f92a96bd) @ 938.58sGroup ID: 250031e3-649a-f505-60e8-0529859f0210Group Index: 0
                                                                            • +
                                                                              74.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Orpheus, rotten seed of great Mother Gith, shame of the Empire.

                                                                              Metadata

                                                                              Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 1f4db2d3-d57d-424c-eb9e-14f29841d5ceSpeaker: 2 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h550ce587g456ag4f00g8a56g18cdedf6e7a3Text UUID: 74ec3266-9141-44e2-b01c-46f55bf91484Group ID: 250031e3-649a-f505-60e8-0529859f0210Group Index: 1Addressed Speaker: 0
                                                                              • +
                                                                                291.Origin_Intro_Laezel: No sight makes me sicker, no name kindles such rage.

                                                                                Metadata

                                                                                Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 7da132b0-1f9f-2ab7-71ec-95709ea068d5Speaker: 2 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h552125e8gf10fg44ffga311g74bfffa1b7d3Text UUID: 5864092c-0210-4cca-95bf-c1ea410aae51Group ID: 250031e3-649a-f505-60e8-0529859f0210Group Index: 2
                                                                                • +
                                                                                  486.Origin_Intro_Laezel: If I'm to end the Netherbrain and its Grand Design, it seems I must call you ally. But I will never call you my prince.

                                                                                  Metadata

                                                                                  Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: db83a313-4a4c-81c8-ab64-43b91a7482b8Speaker: 2 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h987d9e24g8e90g4c02gb04bge7ab6d6e47cbText UUID: 073d4840-2e7f-49dd-aeb7-e47c0419aa37Group ID: 250031e3-649a-f505-60e8-0529859f0210Group Index: 3
                                                                                  • +

                                                                        • +
                                                                          76.Player: Kill Orpheus, and you hand victory to the brain. Is that what you want?

                                                                          Metadata

                                                                          Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 1fc2cb19-f677-1761-5c2a-4e41fe32f041Speaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: h71b84ee8g920fg4e5dga5d4g3813bdc7310aText UUID: 85117b86-890b-44a7-a232-8cfd8a7e17f4Show Once: True
                                                                          • •
                                                                            509.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Vlaakith will not let the Grand Design come to pass! In the Undying Queen, the githyanki are eternal.

                                                                            Metadata

                                                                            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: e36163f2-61ef-a376-3a56-c55d52b8fcfbSpeaker: 2 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h3f7a7430g27cfg4410ga98dg179cba26eac1Text UUID: 0f0ae682-e366-4f2e-91ea-4737cb6c37c5Timeline Phase Index: 72Timeline Dialog Node UUID: e36163f2-61ef-a376-3a56-c55d52b8fcfbTimeline Phase Duration: 10.05sTimeline Phase Shots: 2
                                                                        • +
                                                                          548.Player: You've left me no choice, Lae'zel. I'm striking you down.

                                                                          Metadata

                                                                          Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: f88c3869-cac4-7221-ff78-ef7b188755feSpeaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: h0a4530c7gb41cg4137ga1e6g41aedea1e887Text UUID: e32b1b8a-9877-4a91-94c4-027084980307Show Once: True
                                                                          • +
                                                                            293.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Tsk'va! I see only vermin before me, primed for extermination. Orpheus will not be the only traitor to die this day.

                                                                            Metadata

                                                                            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 7e3e5b86-e8f1-f237-feac-d0846ed0b944Speaker: 2 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h355b73a5g1d23g4ac6g81b8g6ac53eb6b78eText UUID: 6890768c-af4f-42fa-9f42-83e2824058b2Timeline Phase Index: 202Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 7e3e5b86-e8f1-f237-feac-d0846ed0b944Timeline Phase Duration: 17.12sTimeline Phase Shots: 3Group ID: 7e3e5b86-e8f1-f237-feac-d0846ed0b944Group Index: 0
                                                                            • •
                                                                              266.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Let the histories show: Lae'zel of K'liir shattered the Prince of the Comet! [End]END_IllithidOptions_Event_VlaakithPathLaezelBetrayed

                                                                              Flags and Tags

                                                                              settype Global

                                                                        • +
                                                                          480.Player: I am with you, Lae'zel. Today, Orpheus dies.

                                                                          Metadata

                                                                          Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: d71528e3-abdd-90da-d852-1761f6c473ffSpeaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: hc40d08ecg3d67g44d8g8008g2dad8ec98336Text UUID: 805b20b7-8d8d-4159-9961-5419999bc31fShow Once: True
                                                                          • •
                                                                            531.Speaker 0: Slay me then if you can, in the knowledge that if you win, the Netherbrain will take your mind. [End]END_IllithidOptions_State_OrpheusHostileFactionHostileToPlayerGroupAfterDialog

                                                                      • +
                                                                        82.Speaker 0: When the moment of transformation comes, you will be the one ghaik I salute instead of slaughter.                                                                     49.Player: You have given too much already. I will be illithid, I will bear the burden.AvatarLae'zel

                                                                    Flags and Tags

                                                                    checktype Tag | speaker 1 Player

                                                                    Metadata

                                                                    Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 15440ee4-1b14-d773-c625-163bc85f9bb9Speaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: h3023ee91g2022g4e13ga986g690380b6c642Text UUID: 778789fd-c3e0-45d7-8dfa-549815a8d4b8Show Once: True
                                                                    • See Node 329 (Speaker 0) It seems I misjudged you. You were testing my honour. And in so doing, you have convinced me of yours.
                                                                  • +

                                                            551.Speaker 0: We face a Netherbrain. For there to be one way to defeat it is unlikely enough. There will not be another.

                                                              472.Origin_Intro_Karlach: Wait. Maybe you don't have to. It... it should be me.!END_General_State_KarlachOfferedToTransformEND_General_State_KarlachOfferedToTransform

                                                              • +
                                                                15.Player: Why you?

                                                                Metadata

                                                                Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 0a9b801f-e56b-f6c9-d281-8326bfe0657eSpeaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: h89524f40gb629g4c0bg96a7g09130ec4440bText UUID: 3cc4aaa4-46e3-4ac4-923a-f605c2d58698Show Once: True
                                                                • +
                                                                  397.Origin_Intro_Karlach: I'm dying. My heart feels like a live grenade - gonna blow any minute. You still have a life to live. I don't.

                                                                  Metadata

                                                                  Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: aed5fa10-efbc-2739-a516-f2cea08dd259Speaker: 3 (Origin_Intro_Karlach)Text Handle: h77cdfbcfgc921g4135g8099g2d4774d4d1f4Text UUID: bf5a39c3-68b7-4516-97b6-c8e13781c14bTimeline Phase Index: 35Timeline Dialog Node UUID: aed5fa10-efbc-2739-a516-f2cea08dd259Timeline Phase Duration: 13.23sTimeline Phase Shots: 1
                                                                  • +
                                                                    395.Origin_Intro_Karlach: If this is the end for me, let me be the motherfucker who saved the world.

                                                                    Metadata

                                                                    Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: ae9acdef-1453-8c61-8585-974b33cd2f05Speaker: 3 (Origin_Intro_Karlach)Text Handle: h05fc86afgcad4g40f9gb851g073b3c2eae10Text UUID: a5f6763e-c227-418b-99ed-fc89ddc3b710Timeline Phase Index: 33Timeline Dialog Node UUID: ae9acdef-1453-8c61-8585-974b33cd2f05Timeline Phase Duration: 6.01sTimeline Phase Shots: 2
                                                                    • +
                                                                      99.Player: If this is what you really want, all I can say is thank you.END_IllithidOptions_State_AllowKarlachToTransform

                                                                        • +
                                                                          411.Speaker 0: You. Tiefling. You would volunteer your life for the greater good. You would make the ultimate sacrifice. I thank you.

                                                                          Metadata

                                                                          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: b5b18d2f-ef87-5926-dd4f-bed7e6c40c20Speaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: hc4ae2b8eg0559g46ffg936fg407f7d1f1082Text UUID: bac443f2-7d19-4c3e-bd8c-2622fc080089Timeline Phase Index: 22Timeline Dialog Node UUID: b5b18d2f-ef87-5926-dd4f-bed7e6c40c20Timeline Phase Duration: 13.97sTimeline Phase Shots: 4
                                                                          • +
                                                                            341.Speaker 0: The Netherbrain wants nothing more than to see all infected become ghaik. !END_IllithidOptions_Knows_HowOrpheusTransformsUs

                                                                            • +
                                                                              390.Speaker 0: My defences keep the voice of the Absolute out, but just as I can raise them, so I can lower them. !END_IllithidOptions_Knows_HowOrpheusTransformsUs

                                                                              Flags and Tags

                                                                              checktype Dialog | speaker 1 Player
                                                                              • END_IllithidOptions_Knows_HowOrpheusTransformsUs {42edf02f-876b-8927-e8f5-7d9a5fb93423} = False

                                                                              • +
                                                                                179.Speaker 0: I will allow the voice of the Absolute in. Once it reaches you, it will order you to transform.!END_IllithidOptions_Knows_HowOrpheusTransformsUs

                                                                                • +

                                                                                      41.Narrator: *With the withdrawal of Orpheus' power, Karlach's mind is rushed with the full force of the Netherbrain.* note: Orpheus puts two fingers to his temple.                                                                                         103.Narrator: *The very fabric of her being is torn apart and reformed in an agonising process that is over all too soon.*note: Karlach, in excruciating pain and ecstasy, transforms into a mind flayer. (Like gameplay animation, but more cinematic.)

                                                                                        Metadata

                                                                                        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 2bb7dcd3-7c47-c5a6-7e95-8c4a415fa9e4Speaker: -666 (Narrator)Text Handle: h039f3135g46a6g4a17g9398g811eb853ebbcText UUID: cd09f043-8498-4090-8c41-df92f71efd87Group ID: 12a43f43-4a8f-f399-2fcd-50b58417e9aeGroup Index: 1
                                                                                        • +
                                                                                          276.Narrator: *Then - stillness as she stands transformed, once again closed off from the Netherbrain's mind.*note: Karlach stands there as a mind flayer.

                                                                    • +
                                                                      27.Player: No, Karlach. This is for me to do. I'll embrace the parasite - I'll evolve.Avatar7ba0a452-73f7-68dc-cf1e-78c6c117cca5_SelfSacrifice

                                                                      checktype Tag | speaker 1 Player

                                                                      • +
                                                                        5.Origin_Intro_Karlach: I know you. I know you know your own mind. If this is what you want, then all I can say is give 'em Hell, soldier.

                                                                        Metadata

                                                                        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 03ce97c0-9e8a-4f51-237b-1b331c4ef2cbSpeaker: 3 (Origin_Intro_Karlach)Text Handle: h0445a1aag88c5g4c31g80e6g669c35ddc0c1Text UUID: 033c118e-78b1-41a8-b997-f76ebffa870dTimeline Phase Index: 137Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 03ce97c0-9e8a-4f51-237b-1b331c4ef2cbTimeline Phase Duration: 29.87sTimeline Phase Shots: 4Timeline Location Changes: Q Companion position (ff98d926-7773-4e8b-839a-2bc7f92a96bd) @ 1181.41sGroup ID: 03ce97c0-9e8a-4f51-237b-1b331c4ef2cbGroup Index: 0
                                                                        • +
                                                                          297.Speaker 0: Fascinating. You take on the sacrifice, though you could have foisted your responsibility onto another. Perhaps I misjudged you.

                                                                              430.Speaker 0: But your selflessness is misplaced. This is not a sacrifice for you to make - it is a burden for your leader. !Avatar

                                                                              • +
                                                                                199.Speaker 0: My defences keep the voice of the Absolute out, but just as I can raise them, so I can lower them. !END_IllithidOptions_Knows_HowOrpheusTransformsUs

                                                                                • +

                                                                                    • +
                                                                                      505.Speaker 0: You will be the saviour of empires, not least my own. note: [Cody] Orpheus brings his right hand to his temple on "empires," looking down and pausing a beat, before dropping his hand and finishing the line.

                                                                                      Metadata

                                                                                      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: e1b188e9-3624-8118-7076-075876fa44c0Speaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: h566e1b5ag1b6bg4dd0gbd77gd84d9ea03846Text UUID: d4323d43-3a0d-4266-98c3-4912ced1545cTimeline Phase Index: 69Timeline Dialog Node UUID: e1b188e9-3624-8118-7076-075876fa44c0Timeline Phase Duration: 6.96sTimeline Phase Shots: 3
                                                                                      • +

                                                                                        • +
                                                                                          78.Narrator: *You feel a compulsion unlike anything you've ever known - excruciating and exhilarating in equal measure. You wish nothing in the world but to evolve.* note: The player, in excruciating pain and ecstasy, transforms into a mind flayer. (Like gameplay animation, but more cinematic.)

                                                            • +

                                                          384.Speaker 0: Your word means as little to me as his.

                                                          • +
                                                            343.Speaker 0: He may destroy himself when the time comes, if he so chooses, I will have my assurances now.

                                                            Metadata

                                                            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 98d54d8f-e29d-1b25-fbfe-2a7eab218645Speaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: h62a249dbg90deg449eg99afg4bde4798531fText UUID: 1338a351-4a56-483f-80cc-210a8e616328Group ID: abce3be7-3e19-c828-af13-0de232b6af5eGroup Index: 1
                                                            • +
                                                              47.Speaker 0: But I am fortunate... It seems that someone already took the responsibility that you could not.                                                         • +
                                                          328.Speaker 0: Consider, yes, but know that it is not your burden to bear - the weight of it would crush you.!Avatar

                                                          • +

                                                        • +
                                                          219.Speaker 0: Consider, yes. But know that the answer is already foretold. This is the only way to stop the Grand Design.

                                                          Metadata

                                                          • +

                                          • +

                                          • +

                                            Metadata

                                            Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: ab9f37d6-9ba6-204d-7d87-edefe2b9626aSpeaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: h0bdfc47dg8a8fg4854g88bcg6d8d06332311Text UUID: 5f3095d6-16e0-4b2e-ba43-16c1d3600442Show Once: True
                                            • +
                                              305.Speaker 0: ...In this we are aligned. I am obliged to overlook your transgressions.

                                              • +
                                                389.Speaker 0: We will destroy the Netherbrain together, and put a stop to this nascent Empire before it expands into the stars.

                                                Metadata

                                  203.Player: The Emperor held all the cards until now. I freed you at the first opportunity.

                                  Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 53e01b85-e136-587d-3509-184e307ea5b6Speaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: h0e4f5cf4gb048g4500g9323gefb9b3a12813Text UUID: c6f4711a-5fb4-48b8-b171-f53b8167dbbaShow Once: True

                                    356.Speaker 0: That is false.

                                    • +
                                      40.Speaker 0: You had the opportunity to surrender yourself to my honour guard. They would have given you a noble end.

                                      Metadata

                                      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 12984c49-eac4-e978-dbc4-8e6b57e19c85Speaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: ha3c0226egdd04g46edg8223g67e169cacecfText UUID: bdbe9a16-4332-4f81-a961-64bac39dd3e7Timeline Phase Index: 157Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 12984c49-eac4-e978-dbc4-8e6b57e19c85Timeline Phase Duration: 7.23sTimeline Phase Shots: 3
                                      • +
                                        37.Speaker 0: Any worthy individual destined to become ghaik would've done so.

                                        Metadata

                                        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 122eb9b1-a695-d436-9729-83268a269c75Speaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: h3b0d8d6fg20beg476dg97d2gbe59a0053817Text UUID: 7d4aee8d-4910-409c-b190-ea49164a61ebTimeline Phase Index: 144Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 122eb9b1-a695-d436-9729-83268a269c75Timeline Phase Duration: 11.95sTimeline Phase Shots: 3Group ID: 122eb9b1-a695-d436-9729-83268a269c75Group Index: 0
                                        • +
                                          26.Speaker 0: My guard would have freed me, and I would have stopped the elder brain before it evolved into a Netherbrain.

                                          Metadata

                                          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 0edeedfe-8511-fdc4-f90c-cc581f4e32fdSpeaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: h9ce7dc00ge1cfg4511g8e89g6943a65b6ddaText UUID: fb3bd99a-aac8-4e9e-b412-04972956ba4aGroup ID: 122eb9b1-a695-d436-9729-83268a269c75Group Index: 1
                                          • See Node 12 (Speaker 0) All that suffering - avoidable. Were it not for the choices you made.
                                • +
                                  14.Player: Drop the attitude. You're free now, aren't you?

                                  Metadata

                                  Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 09ba3b52-084c-4576-1342-2065fab05633Speaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: h636fb6deg4d52g4611g99c2g254cfdb09378Text UUID: 54b1ce58-da59-42b0-b602-d8715becf7c8Show Once: True
                                  • +
                                    93.Speaker 0: I could have been free much sooner.

                                    Metadata

                                    Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 24fe6c5d-bcf1-c2d4-74f5-0419497e1cd7Speaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: hb7cd09c8gf604g47f5g8390g9e9bbd2eb021Text UUID: fa7720d8-7864-4ece-acd4-c071a4891c4fTimeline Phase Index: 119Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 24fe6c5d-bcf1-c2d4-74f5-0419497e1cd7Timeline Phase Duration: 2.99sTimeline Phase Shots: 1
                                    • See Node 40 (Speaker 0) You had the opportunity to surrender yourself to my honour guard. They would have given you a noble end.
                                • +
                                  166.Player: I should have left you in your chains.

                                  Metadata

                                  Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 433c8347-b3a4-8593-4850-8b47627ff416Speaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: h6b063295g2a30g47begbd79g432c35a31031Text UUID: 5f202889-9247-4bd5-999c-8757b5190fa6Show Once: True
                                  • +
                                    149.Speaker 0: And you should have surrendered yourself to my honour guard. They would have given you a noble end.

                                    Metadata

                                    Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 3d39648e-3139-8d5e-05b5-38f0de62a8dbSpeaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: h3ac4f59bgd16ag401cgb2efg3329d337c2e4Text UUID: 1b350e15-22d1-4202-b7be-c26e16065671Timeline Phase Index: 134Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 3d39648e-3139-8d5e-05b5-38f0de62a8dbTimeline Phase Duration: 6.39sTimeline Phase Shots: 1
                                    • See Node 37 (Speaker 0) Any worthy individual destined to become ghaik would've done so.
                                • +

                          184.Player: You may address me with a little more courtesy. I freed you.

                          Metadata

                          Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 4a76727d-9b72-6c0c-2db4-df6d3a5d858fSpeaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: h84b2a60agefe2g4bb8gb54eg8d7699ad3e2eText UUID: 25aaf123-4074-4036-aa29-eb0d8acad405Show Once: True
                          • +
                            209.Speaker 0: Do not patronise me.

                            Metadata

                            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 56bacdc4-2967-b3c2-4f30-e553100bc309Speaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: hd70a8d2ageaefg449ag897dg7fefde576defText UUID: e7e3ba51-9559-4080-a877-a7bd5b1c5f23Timeline Phase Index: 163Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 56bacdc4-2967-b3c2-4f30-e553100bc309Timeline Phase Duration: 2.37sTimeline Phase Shots: 2Timeline Location Changes: Q Companion position (ff98d926-7773-4e8b-839a-2bc7f92a96bd) @ 1425.13s
                            • +
                              24.Speaker 0: You rejected the illithid when it no longer suited your needs. No doubt you freed me because it suits you now.

                              Metadata

                              Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 0e4f665a-e2e8-b43d-09fb-5c6c28b3ca0eSpeaker: 0 (Speaker 0)Text Handle: h7e449a85g51eeg453bga225g6219d3930834Text UUID: 00fe6f64-ce8e-4f63-9eae-6071343877b7Timeline Phase Index: 143Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 0e4f665a-e2e8-b43d-09fb-5c6c28b3ca0eTimeline Phase Duration: 8.2sTimeline Phase Shots: 2
                              • See Node 381 (Speaker 0) I will neither forgive nor forget your abuse of my powers.
                        • +
                          543.Player: Your honour guard attacked me. What was I supposed to do?

                          Metadata

                          Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: f7835af2-203f-b1d6-02ff-b6ed437ed673Speaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: hb4d4793ag29c5g44f5gbe01gbf6bcd553306Text UUID: fec88094-89c9-420a-a0d7-6d47cfa31778Show Once: True
                          • +
